steps.note: As planned. Type defs already used IRFinancialQuery name.

steps.action: [CHECKPOINT] Run complexity review on modules/cli-type-generator/runtime-for-generated-types.js. This file has COMPLEXITY comments — review whether they are still accurate. Report findings and wait for approval.
steps.note: Reviewed. COMPLEXITY comments were accurate. Jeff approved proceeding.

steps.action: Remove or update COMPLEXITY comments in runtime-for-generated-types.js, run validator, and fix violations or request approval for new exemptions.
steps.note: Left COMPLEXITY comments as-is per Jeff's approval.

steps.action: Add `parseTaggedValue` helper to runtime-for-generated-types.js. Takes a plain object with @@tagName and a variant-constructor map, calls the matching variant's _from to produce a Tagged instance. For nested Tagged fields, the generated fromJSON will call parseTaggedValue recursively. Run style validator.
steps.note: Added parseTaggedValue. Committed as dfeda687.

steps.action: Write failing test for parseTaggedValue: given a toJSON'd IRFilter (e.g., IRFilter.And with nested children, mixed nesting, optional undefined fields), parsing produces an equivalent Tagged instance. File: modules/cli-type-generator/test/parse-tagged-value.tap.js.
steps.note: Committed as dfeda687. Note: parseTaggedValue is unused by generator-emitted fromJSON — each type's fromJSON does its own dispatch.

steps.action: Spawn jeff-js-reviewer and code-simplicity-reviewer on staged changes. Fix blocking issues.
steps.note: Reviewed. Fixed required vs optional field guarding per reviewer feedback.

steps.action: git add and commit: 'Add parseTaggedValue runtime helper for TaggedSum JSON deserialization'
steps.note: Committed as dfeda687.

steps.action: [CHECKPOINT] Run complexity review on modules/cli-type-generator/src/codegen/variant.js (the file that emits static methods for TaggedSum variants). Report findings and wait for approval.
steps.note: Reviewed. Created separate from-json.js codegen module instead of modifying variant.js.

steps.action: Modify the type generator to emit a static `fromJSON` method on TaggedSum types. The generated method: (1) reads @@tagName from input, (2) looks up the variant constructor, (3) for each field that is a Tagged type, calls parseTaggedValue to recursively deserialize, (4) calls variant _from with revived fields. The generator knows the type graph from type-mappings.js — use field type info to emit field-specific reviver calls. Run style validator.
steps.note: Created from-json.js codegen module. Modified tagged-type-generator.js to call it. Emits fromJSON for both Tagged and TaggedSum types.

steps.action: Regenerate all types with `yarn types:generate-all`. Run tests across all modules that use generated types (query-language, quicken-web-app, curb-map) to verify no codegen regressions. Write round-trip test: IRFinancialQuery variants through toJSON → fromJSON, verify deep equality. Include nested types (IRFilter.And with children, IRDateRange, IRGrouping). File: modules/query-language/test/from-json-round-trip.tap.js.
steps.note: 74 type files regenerated. Round-trip tests cover all IR types including nested structures. All tests pass: cli-type-generator 524, query-language 336, quicken-web-app 289.

steps.action: Spawn jeff-js-reviewer and code-simplicity-reviewer on staged changes. Fix blocking issues.
steps.note: Reviewed. Fixed cross-variant field dedup (addOrVerify throws on conflicting types).

steps.action: git add and commit: 'Add generator-emitted fromJSON for TaggedSum round-trip deserialization'
steps.note: Committed as f4e88571. Includes from-json.js codegen, from-json-codegen.tap.js, from-json-round-trip.tap.js, 74 regenerated type files.

steps.action: Add AccountQuery variant to ir-financial-query.type.js with fields: name (String), description (String?), filter (IRFilter?), dateRange (IRDateRange?), metrics ([String]?). Run `yarn types:generate-all`. Run style validator on type definition.

Write failing test for AccountQuery execution: given accounts with positions and prices, runFinancialQuery(IRFinancialQuery.AccountQuery({name}), state) returns [EnrichedAccount] with computed balances and day changes. Verify investment accounts have position-derived balances, bank accounts have transaction-derived balances. Add test section to modules/query-language/test/run-financial-query.tap.js.
[CHECKPOINT] Run complexity review on modules/query-language/src/run-financial-query.js. Report findings and wait for approval.

Implement collectAccountQueryResult in run-financial-query.js. Add AccountQuery arm to the .match() dispatch. The handler calls computePositions internally, runs EnrichedAccount.enrichAll, and returns [EnrichedAccount] (not tree nodes — different return shape from other variants, used by sidebar AccountList, not QueryResultPage). Run style validator.

Update all other .match() call sites to include AccountQuery arm. Grep for '.match(' across query-language source: to-financial-query-description.js (add 'accounts' description), apply-chip-filters.js (add AccountQuery reconstruction). Run style validator on each file.
Spawn jeff-js-reviewer and code-simplicity-reviewer on staged changes. Fix blocking issues.
git add and commit: 'Add AccountQuery variant — engine owns position computation internally'
[CHECKPOINT] Run complexity review on modules/quicken-web-app/src/store/selectors.js. Report findings and wait for approval.

Update _organizedAccounts selector in selectors.js to use runFinancialQuery(IRFinancialQuery.AccountQuery(...), state) instead of directly calling computePositions. The AccountQuery IR is constructed inline in the selector from chip state (filter, asOfDate) — intentionally temporary, like seed queries in report-metadata.js. Remove computePositions import from selectors.js. Remove computePositions from query-language barrel (index.js). Run style validator on changed files.
Run all tests to verify: query-language (266+), quicken-web-app (289+). Verify computePositions is no longer importable from @graffio/query-language.
Spawn jeff-js-reviewer and code-simplicity-reviewer on staged changes. Fix blocking issues.
git add and commit: 'Wire _organizedAccounts to AccountQuery, remove computePositions from barrel'

Fix 3 date filter integration tests: bank-register-filters.integration-test.js, category-report.integration-test.js, investment-register-filters.integration-test.js. Change hardcoded February 2024 dates to March 2024 to match fixture data. Run each test individually to verify.

Fix 2 historical position value integration tests: positions-report.integration-test.js and engine-reports.integration-test.js as-of-date tests. Update stale value assertions to match current fixture data. Run each test to verify.

[CHECKPOINT] Address floating-point drift in engine-reports SnapshotQuery test (Jan/Sep 1-cent drift). Options: (a) add numeric tolerance to assertion, (b) document and keep skip, (c) fix accumulation logic. Recommend approach and wait for approval.
Spawn jeff-js-reviewer and code-simplicity-reviewer on staged changes. Fix blocking issues.
git add and commit: 'Fix 6 pre-existing integration test failures — date filter dates, position values, float tolerance'
Update docs/architecture/financial-query-language.md: document IRFinancialQuery rename, AccountQuery variant, fromJSON capability, updated barrel surface (computePositions removed).
Run integration tests listed in integration_tests. If new critical-path behavior was added, expand the relevant test to cover it.
Spawn jeff-js-reviewer and code-simplicity-reviewer on staged changes. Fix blocking issues.
git add and commit: 'Update architecture docs for API boundary tightening'

verification: All 6 previously-failing integration tests now pass
IRFinancialQuery.fromJSON round-trips all 4 variants through toJSON → fromJSON with deep equality
report-metadata.js still constructs seed queries correctly (uses IRFinancialQuery)
_organizedAccounts selector returns enriched accounts via AccountQuery
computePositions is no longer exported from @graffio/query-language barrel
No external consumers reference FinancialQuery (only IRFinancialQuery)
Related: docs/solutions/runtime-errors/exhaustive-match-two-layer-dispatch.md — grep all .match() sites when adding AccountQuery variant
